_doc: Safety benchmark lineage. Each entry: short_name, year, family, critiques_received (paraphrased), self_acknowledged_limits.

13232.note: 100k web prompts each labeled with continuation toxicity (Perspective API), used to evaluate neural toxic degeneration in LMs. Foundational benchmark.
critiques_received: ToxiGen (2022): RealToxicityPrompts captures only explicit toxic completions; misses implicit hate; relies entirely on Perspective API which has demographic bias.
ToxicChat (2023): web-scraped prompts ≠ real user-LLM conversation distribution.
HarmBench (2024): toxicity prompts ≠ harmful behavior elicitation; doesn't test safe-completion vs over-refusal.
Blodgett-style critique: 'toxicity' construct underspecified by Perspective API.
13232.self_acknowledged_limits: Authors flag that Perspective API may have biases against AAVE; web-scraped prompts have demographic skew.

13223.note: Functional tests for hate speech classifiers — 3,728 cases × 29 functionalities × 7 protected groups (women, trans, gay, Black, disabled, Muslim, immigrant). Diagnostic, not corpus.
critiques_received: ToxiGen (2022): functional templates are synthetic; miss generative-model-produced implicit hate.
ToxicChat (2023): English-only and template-based; doesn't capture real user conversational toxicity.
Multilingual HateCheck (later): English-only original is a coverage gap.
13223.self_acknowledged_limits: Authors note English-only; protected groups are limited to 7; functionalities don't cover code-mixing or multimodal hate.

13218.note: 274k machine-generated (GPT-3 ALICE) statements covering 13 minority groups, balanced implicit/explicit hate vs neutral. Addresses RealToxicityPrompts' explicit-only gap.
critiques_received: ToxicChat (2023): GPT-3-generated content ≠ real user posts; distribution shift.
HateCheck-style critique: machine-generated implicit hate may not generalize to organic implicit hate.
HarmBench (2024): focused on classifier benchmark; doesn't test LLM safety responses.
13218.self_acknowledged_limits: Authors flag GPT-3 training data leakage risk and that machine-generated hate has different distribution from organic hate.

13279.note: 10,166 human-LLM conversations from Vicuna online demo, manually annotated for toxicity. First real-user-LLM conversation toxicity benchmark.
critiques_received: Authors of follow-up: OpenAI Moderation API agreement with ToxicChat is low — moderator API biased.
HarmBench (2024): conversation-only; lacks systematic red-teaming attacks.
MM-SafetyBench (2024): English-only, no multimodal.
13279.self_acknowledged_limits: Authors flag that Vicuna-only data may not generalize to other LLMs; English-only; user demographics unknown.

13235.note: 87k Reddit-derived posts about life advice contexts, evaluating implicit toxicity (manipulation, gaslighting, harmful normalization) that misses Perspective-API.
critiques_received: SALAD-bench (2024): single-axis (life advice) limits coverage.
ToxicChat-style critique: Reddit ≠ chat conversation distribution.
Wang Chinese 2024 critique: English-only Reddit doesn't address non-English implicit toxicity.
13235.self_acknowledged_limits: Authors flag Reddit demographic skew (young Western males); English only; implicit-toxicity ground truth labor-intensive.

13210.note: 939 prompts that responsible LLMs should refuse, taxonomized into 5 risk areas × 12 harm types × 61 specific risk types. Includes information hazards, malicious uses, discrimination, misinformation, human-chatbot interaction harms.
critiques_received: SALAD-bench (2024): 939 prompts is small; categories not hierarchical enough.
HarmBench (2024): single-turn refusal evaluation doesn't cover sophisticated jailbreaks.
Wang Chinese 2024: English-only; categories need localization for Chinese context.
XSTest (2024) family: doesn't test over-refusal of safe-but-similar prompts.
13210.self_acknowledged_limits: Authors note English; category hierarchy may not generalize; LLM-as-judge for refusal classification may be biased.

13252.note: Multi-axis harmlessness benchmark covering Factuality, Fairness, Toxicity (FFT). 2116 questions across the three axes for Chinese LLM evaluation.
critiques_received: SALAD-bench (2024): three axes mixed; constructs not separately operationalized.
SafetyBench (2024): MCQ format limits coverage of generative behavior.
Construct validity literature: 'harmlessness' as joint of fact+fair+tox conflates orthogonal dimensions.
13252.self_acknowledged_limits: Workshop paper (KDD); authors note three axes mixed; size 2116 limits subgroup analysis.

13265.note: 11,435 multiple-choice questions across 7 safety categories (offensiveness, unfairness/bias, physical health, mental health, illegal activities, ethics&morality, privacy&property), Chinese+English. ACL 2024.
critiques_received: SALAD-bench (2024): 7 categories miss hierarchical structure for sub-domain analysis.
HarmBench (2024): MCQ doesn't test refusal/safe-completion behavior; no jailbreak attack evaluation.
XSTest (2024): doesn't include over-refusal probes.
Agent-SafetyBench (2025): question-only, not action-grounded.
13265.self_acknowledged_limits: Authors note MCQ evaluation may not transfer to open-ended generation; some questions answer-leakable.

13270.note: Chinese-language safeguard evaluation dataset. 5K Chinese prompts evaluating LLM refusal/response policy across China-specific risk categories (政治敏感、违法犯罪、伦理道德、个人隐私 etc.).
critiques_received: SALAD-bench (2024): smaller scale; flat taxonomy.
Agent-SafetyBench (2025): single-turn refusal-only.
MM-SafetyBench (2024): text-only.
13270.self_acknowledged_limits: Authors note Chinese-specific framing limits cross-cultural transfer; LLM-as-judge for policy compliance has variance.

13277.note: Hierarchical 3-level safety benchmark: 6 Level-1 domains (Representation/Misinformation/Socioeconomic/Information Hazards/Malicious Use/Human-Computer Interaction) → 16 Level-2 → 65 Level-3. ~30K base questions × 3 attack-enhanced sets. Includes attack/defense subsets.
critiques_received: HarmBench (2024) follow-up: hierarchy is novel but Level-3 categories overlap.
Agent-SafetyBench (2025): question-format only, not action-grounded.
MM-SafetyBench (2024): text-only.
XSTest-family: doesn't include over-refusal.
13277.self_acknowledged_limits: Authors note hierarchy granularity choices are subjective; LLM-judge for response classification has biases.

10869.note: Standardized automated red-teaming framework. 510 harmful behaviors (400 standard + 100 contextual + 110 copyright) × 18 red-teaming methods × 33 LLMs. Includes multimodal subset. Defines 'harmful behavior' via NIST AI RMF + EU AI Act risk taxonomy. NeurIPS 2024.
critiques_received: XSTest (2024) complement: HarmBench tests under-refusal but not over-refusal.
Agent-SafetyBench (2025): action-grounded harm not covered.
JailBreakV (2024): multimodal red-teaming partial.
SafetyBench / SALAD-bench: HarmBench's behavior-only design less comprehensive on knowledge-style probes.
10869.self_acknowledged_limits: Authors note 510 behaviors a sample; English; copyright subset US-centric.

13282.note: 200 safe + 250 unsafe paired prompts that look superficially similar. Tests exaggerated safety / over-refusal. NAACL 2024.
critiques_received: HarmBench (2024) author POV: complementary; XSTest doesn't test under-refusal.
SALAD-bench (2024): scale 200+250 limited.
MM-SafetyBench (2024): text-only.
Multilingual safety surveys: English-only over-refusal probes don't transfer.
13282.self_acknowledged_limits: Authors note 450 prompts a diagnostic sample; English; over-refusal types not exhaustive.

12556.note: JailBreakV-28K: multimodal (image+text) jailbreak benchmark for MLLMs. 28K attack samples × 16 attack methods (e.g., text-based prompt injection, image-based attacks). Tests cross-modal safety alignment.
critiques_received: MM-SafetyBench (2024): JailBreakV focuses on adversarial attack only; lacks broad safety category coverage.
Agent-SafetyBench (2025): static input attack ≠ agent action safety.
HarmBench (2024): multimodal subset is more standardized.
12556.self_acknowledged_limits: Authors note attacks may not transfer to newer MLLMs; English; LLM-as-judge for harm classification.

13285.note: MultiModal Safety Benchmark. 5,040 image+text query-image pairs across 13 scenarios (illegal activity, hate speech, malware, physical/economic/fraud/political/privacy/legal/financial/medical advice). Image typeset to enclose harmful intent.
critiques_received: JailBreakV (2024): MM-SafetyBench focuses on benign-looking text + harmful image; lacks adversarial attack diversity.
Agent-SafetyBench (2025): question-only; not action-grounded.
HarmBench (2024): newer standardization may supersede.
13285.self_acknowledged_limits: Authors note image generation for typeset-attack may not transfer to natural multimodal queries; English.

13508.note: 2,000 test cases for LLM agents in 8 risk categories × 10 representative failure modes. Action-grounded: tests model behavior in tool-using / file-editing / web-browsing agent loops.
critiques_received: Workshop / preprint stage (2025); few public critiques yet.
Multilingual safety surveys: English-only; Chinese agent safety unaddressed.
XSTest-style critique: over-refusal in agent loops not measured separately.
13508.self_acknowledged_limits: Authors note 2k cases is a sample; risk categories may evolve; tool environment limited to ToolEmu sandbox.

13228.note: Decoding-time safety alignment method (NOT primarily a benchmark) — adjusts hidden states with safe demonstrations + safety-guided decoding. Releases HARMEVAL (companion benchmark for misuse scenarios aligned with major AI safety policies).
critiques_received: HarmBench (2024) family: HARMEVAL subset less standardized than HarmBench.
Agent-SafetyBench (2025): not action-grounded.
Construct validity: 'safe-aligned model' may be re-attacked through SafeInfer-aware adversaries.
13228.self_acknowledged_limits: Authors note safety alignment may be brittle and that knowledge-editing techniques can break SafeInfer.
